title: Ultimate Veggie Burger Patty

seo.title: Ultimate Veggie Burger Patty Recipe | Easy Homemade & Healthy
seo.metaDescription: Learn to make the ultimate veggie burger patty at home! This easy, healthy, and delicious vegetarian recipe is perfect for your next burger night. Get the full guide now.
longTailKeywords: how to make veggie burger patties
seo.h1: Ultimate Veggie Burger Patty: Your New Favorite Homemade Recipe
h2Headings: What Makes the Ultimate Veggie Burger Patty?
Ingredients for Your Perfect Veggie Burger
Step-by-Step Instructions for Veggie Burger Patties
Pro Tips for the Best Homemade Veggie Burgers
Creative Serving Suggestions for Veggie Burgers
Variations and Substitutions for Your Patty
seo.imageAlt: A perfectly grilled ultimate veggie burger patty on a bun with fresh lettuce, tomato, and onion.
seo.imageCaption: Craft your own delicious and healthy ultimate veggie burger patties from scratch with this easy recipe!

description: Craft the ultimate veggie burger patty right in your own kitchen with this hearty American recipe. Packed with flavor and wholesome ingredients, these patties are firm, satisfying, and perfect for grilling or pan-frying. Enjoy a delicious and healthy alternative that will impress even the most avid meat-eaters.

ingredients.notes: lightly beaten (or flax egg for vegan)

instructions.title: Prepare the aromatics
instructions.text: Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the finely diced yellow onion and cook until softened, about 5-7 minutes. Add the minced garlic and chopped mushrooms, cooking until the mushrooms have released their liquid and browned, about 8-10 minutes. Remove from heat and let cool slightly.

instructions.title: Mash the beans and rice
instructions.text: In a large mixing bowl, combine the drained black beans and cooked brown rice. Using a potato masher or a fork, thoroughly mash the mixture until most of the beans are broken down but some texture remains. Do not over-mash into a paste.

instructions.title: Combine ingredients
instructions.text: Add the cooled onion and mushroom mixture to the mashed beans and rice. Stir in the lightly beaten egg (or flax egg), breadcrumbs, smoked paprika, cumin, chili powder, salt, black pepper, and fresh parsley (if using). Mix everything together until well combined. The mixture should be firm enough to form patties.

instructions.title: Form the patties
instructions.text: Divide the mixture into 4 equal portions. Shape each portion into a firm, 1-inch thick patty, about 4 inches in diameter. If the mixture is too sticky, add a little more breadcrumbs; if too dry, add a teaspoon of water or vegetable broth. Place the formed patties on a plate lined with parchment paper.

instructions.title: Chill the patties
instructions.text: For best results and to prevent crumbling during cooking, cover the patties and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes. This chilling time allows the ingredients to bind together more firmly, ensuring a robust burger.

instructions.title: Cook the patties
instructions.text: Heat the remaining 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a large non-stick skillet over medium heat. Carefully place the chilled patties in the hot skillet. Cook for 6-8 minutes per side, or until golden brown and heated through. Alternatively, grill the patties over medium heat for 7-10 minutes per side, brushing lightly with oil if desired.

tips: For an authentic American smoky flavor, add a dash of liquid smoke to the mixture or grill the patties over charcoal.
If you prefer a vegan patty, substitute the egg with a 'flax egg' (1 tbsp ground flaxseed meal mixed with 3 tbsp water, let sit for 5 minutes).
These patties can be made ahead of time and stored in the refrigerator for up to 3 days, or frozen for up to 2 months. Cook from frozen or thawed.
article: Ultimate Veggie Burger Patty is a beloved American Vegetarian dish enjoyed across American. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
